rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Matryoshka Representation Learning (NeurIPS 2022)
results.snippet: LLM-side: nested hierarchical embeddings; prefix-valid; coarse-to-fine; 14x smaller at same accuracy.

results.title: MatryoshkaLora (2026/5)
results.snippet: LLM-side: hierarchical low-rank representations for LLM fine-tuning.

results.title: Matryoshka-Adaptor (2024/7)
results.snippet: LLM-side: transforms arbitrary embeddings into matryoshka.

results.title: Matryoshka Multimodal Models (2024/5)
results.snippet: LLM-side: nested visual tokens at multiple granularities.

results.title: Temporal-aware Matryoshka Adaptation (2026/1)
results.snippet: LLM-side: temporal IR with matryoshka adaptation.

results.title: MRL behind OpenAI text-embedding-3 (Zilliz)
results.snippet: LLM-side: OpenAI/Gemini APIs ship matryoshka embeddings to millions of users.

fail_reason: 7 of 8 results are hits (ranks 2-8). All 7 hits clear both the semantic threshold (sem>=0.7) and the functional threshold (func>=0.7) against the canonical paraphrase 'MATRY-LLM — nested prefix-valid coarse-to-fine token embedding for LLMs'. The candidate mechanism (Matryoshka Representation Learning: nested coarse-to-fine, prefix-valid embedding) is identical to arxiv:2205.13147 (judge_score=1.00) and is widely extended (MatryoshkaLora, Matryoshka-Adaptor, Matryoshka Multimodal, Beyond-Matryoshka, Temporal Matryoshka) and shipped in production (OpenAI text-embedding-3 via MRL). Functional collision is total; the candidate exhibits anti-novelty.
